paper_focus.title: Temperature dependence of the deformation behavior and mechanical properties of a Fe–Mn–Al–C low-density steel for cryogenic application
paper_focus.research_objective: To investigate the temperature dependence of deformed microstructures, work hardening behavior, and mechanical properties of an Fe–20Mn–6Al-0.6C-0.15Si austenitic low-density steel, providing theoretical support for its cryogenic application.
main_process_variables: melting and casting in vacuum induction furnace, air cooled to RT
tensile test at −196 °C, −77 °C and room temperature
Charpy impact test at −196 °C, −77 °C and room temperature
test temperature (as primary variable controlling SFE and deformation mechanisms)
